title: Refract
summary: A session-centered research workspace that keeps literature review, saved evidence, datasets, and staged statistical analysis inside one continuous environment.
role: Builder of the session model, evidence architecture, and the qualitative-to-quantitative workflow that ties reading, comparison, and analysis together.

links.label: Workflow article
links.description: Companion write-up on the design logic behind the workspace.

pageSections.title: Why the project matters
pageSections.markdown:
Refract treats the research session as the continuity layer. Papers, saved evidence, comparison outputs, datasets, and model runs all stay inside one shared context so interpretation does not have to be reconstructed after the fact.

That matters because applied research work usually gets split across PDF readers, note systems, notebooks, spreadsheets, and draft documents. Refract is designed to collapse that fragmentation into one working environment.

pageSections.title: The session connects qualitative and quantitative work
pageSections.description: The implementation is organized around a two-track flow: literature ingestion, reading, and comparison on one side; session-scoped dataset upload, profiling, and modeling on the other. Both tracks converge in literature-informed interpretation.
pageSections.caption: README workflow diagram from the live repository.

items.label: Passage-grounded reading
items.context: Upload PDFs, highlight passages, and respond from the active paper.

items.label: Persistent research memory
items.context: Saved questions, notes, concept tags, and source-linked responses.

items.label: Cross-paper synthesis
items.context: Matrices, topic coverage, and gap analysis tied to a live session goal.

items.label: Session-scoped analysis
items.context: Dataset profile, audit, model benchmarking, and evidence-qualified interpretation.

pageSections.title: The UI is built around research work, not generic chat
pageSections.description: The Reader, Compare, and Stats views are separate surfaces, but they are joined by the same session object. The design goal is continuity: keep evidence, assumptions, and analysis outputs visible in context rather than scattered across tools.
pageSections.caption: Reader workspace from the live repository README.

pageSections.title: The architecture reflects the workflow split
pageSections.description: The live implementation uses a React frontend, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL state, ChromaDB vector storage, and object storage for uploaded files and artifacts. The important seam is the research session model, which coordinates both the literature and analysis tracks.
pageSections.caption: System architecture diagram from the repository README.

pageSections.quote: The strongest implemented thread is not a single model. It is the integrated workflow that keeps reading, comparison, evidence, and statistical analysis in the same frame.

github.sourceDescription: Repository-backed source material for the Refract project page.
github.importedReadmeMarkdown: README content can be refreshed automatically during builds. This copy is intentionally retained as a repository-sourced appendix beneath the curated project narrative.
